walkthrough.title: Create the Desert Grid
walkthrough.text: Let's set up Forbidden Desert! First, find all 24 desert tiles. Shuffle them face down and arrange them in a five by five grid, leaving the center space empty. That empty center space is the storm's eye, the starting point of the sandstorm. All tiles start face down with their sandy side up.

walkthrough.title: Place Initial Sand Markers
walkthrough.text: Now grab the sand markers from the supply. Place one sand marker on each of the eight tiles directly surrounding the center empty space. That is the ring of tiles adjacent to the storm eye. These tiles are already partially buried and will need clearing before they can be excavated.

walkthrough.title: Set the Storm Meter
walkthrough.text: Find the storm meter and its clip. Choose your difficulty level. Novice starts at two, Normal at three, Elite at four, and Legendary at five. The storm meter determines how many storm cards you draw each turn. For your first game, we recommend starting at Novice. Place the clip at your chosen level.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 31 storm cards and place them face down as a draw pile. These cards drive the storm and can be devastating. Separately, shuffle the equipment cards and place them face down near the board. Equipment cards are your lifeline, so keep them accessible.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Machine Parts
walkthrough.text: Place the four flying machine parts near the board where everyone can reach them. You need all four: the Propeller, the Engine, the Navigation Deck, and the Crystal. Finding and assembling these parts is how you win the game.

walkthrough.title: Assign Roles
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the six adventurer cards and deal one to each player. Each role has a unique power and a different starting water level. The Explorer can move diagonally. The Meteorologist can manage storm cards. The Navigator can move other players. The Water Carrier starts with extra water and can share it. The Climber can cross blocked tiles. The Archaeologist clears sand faster.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Players
walkthrough.text: Each player takes their matching pawn and places their water meter clip at the maximum water level shown on their adventurer card. All players place their pawns in the center of the grid, which is the crash site where the storm eye is. The thirstiest player goes first, or choose randomly. You are ready to begin.

bullets: Shuffle 24 desert tiles face down
Arrange in 5x5 grid, center space empty (storm eye)
All tiles start face down (sand side up)

bullets: Place 1 sand marker on each of the 8 tiles surrounding the center
These tiles are partially buried from the start

summary.title: Set the Storm Meter
bullets: Choose difficulty: Novice (2), Normal (3), Elite (4), Legendary (5)
Storm meter = # storm cards drawn per turn
Recommended: start at Novice for first game

bullets: Shuffle 31 storm cards → face-down draw pile
Shuffle equipment cards → face-down pile nearby

summary.title: Set Out the Machine Parts
bullets: Place 4 parts near board: Propeller, Engine, Navigation Deck, Crystal
Collecting all 4 is the win condition

summary.title: Assign Roles
bullets: Shuffle 6 adventurer cards, deal 1 per player
Each role has unique power and different starting water
Explorer (diagonal), Meteorologist (storm management), Navigator (move others), Water Carrier (extra water), Climber (cross blocked), Archaeologist (clear 2 sand)

summary.title: Set Up Players
bullets: Each player: take pawn, set water clip to max
All pawns start at center (crash site / storm eye)
Thirstiest player goes first (or random)

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you do two things. First, take up to four actions. You can choose from moving, removing sand, excavating tiles, or picking up machine parts. You can take fewer than four actions if you want. Second, draw storm cards equal to the current storm meter level and resolve each one immediately. Then pass to the next player clockwise.

walkthrough.title: Action: Move
walkthrough.text: You can move up, down, left, or right, but not diagonally unless you are the Explorer. You cannot move onto a tile with two or more sand markers because it is blocked, unless you are the Climber. Excavated tunnel tiles let you travel between any two tunnel entrances as a single move action.

walkthrough.title: Action: Remove Sand
walkthrough.text: A tile must have zero sand markers before it can be excavated. The Archaeologist is extra powerful here, removing two sand markers per action instead of one. Sand builds up quickly, so clearing it is a constant priority.

walkthrough.title: Action: Excavate
walkthrough.text: Spend one action to flip a face-down tile that you are standing on, as long as it has zero sand markers. Excavating reveals what is underneath: gear tiles give you equipment cards, water tiles restore water, tunnel tiles provide shelter and fast travel, clue tiles help locate machine parts, and the launch pad is where you escape.

walkthrough.title: Finding Machine Parts
walkthrough.text: Each of the four machine parts has two clue tiles. One clue shows the row and the other shows the column. When both clue tiles for a part are excavated, the part appears at the tile where the row and column intersect. A player standing on that tile can pick it up. Finding parts requires excavating clues, not digging randomly.

walkthrough.title: Storm Cards: Wind
walkthrough.text: When drawn, tiles shift in the indicated direction. The storm eye stays fixed while tiles slide around it. One, two, or three tiles shift depending on the card. After tiles shift, place one sand marker on each tile that just moved. This is how sand piles up across the desert.

walkthrough.title: Storm Cards: Sun Beats Down
walkthrough.text: When a Sun Beats Down card is drawn, every player not on a tunnel tile or protected by a Solar Shield loses one water. No tiles shift. This is the most dangerous card type because it drains water from everyone simultaneously. Staying near tunnel tiles or holding Solar Shields is your best defense.

walkthrough.title: Storm Cards: Storm Picks Up
walkthrough.text: This means future turns draw more storm cards, accelerating the danger. No tiles shift on this card either. Each Storm Picks Up card makes the game permanently harder. There is no way to reverse the storm meter.

walkthrough.title: Equipment Cards
walkthrough.text: These are powerful tools. The Dune Blaster removes all sand from one tile. The Jet Pack moves you or another player to any unblocked tile. The Terrascope lets you peek at a face-down tile. The Solar Shield protects from Sun Beats Down. The Secret Water Reserve gives two water to any player. The Time Throttle grants two extra actions. Equipment does not cost an action to use and can be played on any player's turn.

walkthrough.title: Water Management
walkthrough.text: Water is your most precious resource. Each player tracks water on their adventurer card. When water reaches the skull, that player dies and everyone loses. The Water Carrier can share water with adjacent players as an action and can revisit excavated well tiles for more water. Protect the Water Carrier and keep them mobile.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: You win by gathering all four machine parts and getting every player to the launch pad tile. Then you escape the desert together. You lose if any player's water reaches the skull symbol, if a sand marker must be placed but the supply is empty, or if the storm meter reaches the skull at the top. This is a cooperative game with no individual scoring. You win or lose as a team.

Resolve each storm card immediately → pass clockwise

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Desert
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through several turns with an example team to show you how actions, storm cards, and teamwork combine. Your team has crash-landed in the desert and must find four machine parts to escape. Follow along even if your roles are different. Ready? Let's survive.

walkthrough.title: Meet the Team
walkthrough.text: Our example team has four players. The Explorer, who can move and remove sand diagonally. The Water Carrier, who starts with five water and can share it. The Archaeologist, who removes two sand per action. And the Navigator, who can move other players. Everyone starts at the crash site in the center of the grid.

walkthrough.title: Explorer's First Turn
walkthrough.text: The Explorer goes first. They spend their first action removing sand from an adjacent tile. Second action, they remove sand from another adjacent tile. Third action, they move onto the now-cleared tile. Fourth action, they excavate it, revealing a clue tile for the Engine! Now the Explorer draws storm cards. Two wind cards shift tiles and pile more sand.

walkthrough.title: Water Carrier's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Water Carrier moves toward a different section of the grid, clearing sand along the way. They excavate a water tile, restoring water for themselves and nearby players. The Water Carrier is the team's lifeline. Keep them moving to wells and distributing water.

walkthrough.title: Archaeologist's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Archaeologist shines at clearing sand. They remove two sand markers per action, so in four actions they can clear eight sand markers. The Archaeologist clears a path toward an unexplored section of the grid and excavates a gear tile, drawing an equipment card: a Jet Pack! Powerful tool saved for later.

walkthrough.title: Navigator's Turn
walkthrough.text: The Navigator has a unique ability: they can move other players up to three spaces as a single action. The Navigator uses this to reposition the Water Carrier closer to a thirsty teammate. Then the Navigator excavates their own tile, finding another clue. Two clues for the Engine are now revealed, and the Engine part appears on the board!

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Ignoring Sand Buildup
walkthrough.text: After several turns, the team notices a problem. Sand has been piling up on the east side of the grid because no one has been clearing it. Three tiles now have three or four sand markers each. They are completely blocked. Even the Archaeologist would need two full turns just to clear one tile. The team has let sand get out of control.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Clear Sand Early and Often
walkthrough.text: Sand buildup is the number one way teams lose Forbidden Desert. Once a tile has three or four sand markers, it costs most of a turn just to clear it. Address sand early when it is one or two markers. Assign the Archaeologist to the most sand-heavy areas and do not let any tile go above two markers if you can help it.

walkthrough.title: Sun Beats Down!
walkthrough.text: The next storm card drawn is Sun Beats Down. Every player not on a tunnel tile loses one water. The Explorer and Archaeologist are exposed. They each drop one water level. The Navigator is on a tunnel tile and is safe. The Water Carrier uses their ability to share water with the Explorer nearby. Sun Beats Down is devastating to spread-out teams.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Splitting Too Far Apart
walkthrough.text: The team realizes the Explorer is three tiles away from the nearest teammate and nearly out of water. If another Sun Beats Down hits, the Explorer could die. The team split up to cover more ground but forgot about water logistics. The Water Carrier cannot reach the Explorer in time without the Navigator's help.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Stay Within Water Range
walkthrough.text: Never let a player wander so far from the team that the Water Carrier cannot reach them within one or two turns. Communication is everything in a cooperative game. Before each turn, discuss as a team where everyone needs to be. Keep the Water Carrier central and mobile.

walkthrough.title: The Mistake: Hoarding Equipment
walkthrough.text: The Archaeologist has been holding the Jet Pack for five turns, waiting for the perfect moment. Meanwhile, the Explorer nearly died from dehydration and the Jet Pack could have moved them to a tunnel for safety. Equipment cards are powerful and time-sensitive. Waiting for the perfect play often means losing before you use them.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Use Equipment Aggressively
walkthrough.text: Equipment cards do not cost an action to play. There is no reason to hoard them when a teammate is in danger. Use the Jet Pack to rescue stranded players. Use the Dune Blaster to clear sand emergencies. Use the Solar Shield before Sun Beats Down decimates your water. Play equipment early and often.

walkthrough.title: Collecting Parts and Heading to the Launch Pad
walkthrough.text: The team recovers and works together. They excavate remaining clue tiles, locate all four machine parts, and collect them. Now everyone needs to converge on the launch pad tile. The Navigator moves teammates efficiently. The Water Carrier keeps everyone hydrated. The Archaeologist clears a path through the sand.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen how Forbidden Desert's mechanics work together: actions, storm cards, water management, and team coordination. You watched three common mistakes. Remember: clear sand early, stay within water range, and use equipment aggressively. You are ready to brave the desert for real!

walkthrough.title: Before You Explore
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for cooperative strategy basics and role-specific advice.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to tile types, equipment cards, and role powers.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the most common mistakes teams make. First, letting sand pile up to three or four markers before addressing it. Second, splitting too far apart so the Water Carrier cannot help thirsty teammates. Third, hoarding equipment cards instead of using them when needed. Fourth, chasing machine parts before both clue tiles are revealed. And fifth, forgetting to use tunnel tiles for shelter from Sun Beats Down cards.

walkthrough.title: Prioritize Clue Tiles
walkthrough.text: You cannot find machine parts until both clue tiles for each part are excavated. This means your primary goal is uncovering clue tiles as fast as possible. Gear tiles and water tiles are nice bonuses, but clue tiles are what actually progress the win condition. When deciding which direction to explore, prioritize paths toward unexcavated tiles rather than clearing sand on already-known locations.

walkthrough.title: Sand Management
walkthrough.text: Sand is the silent killer in Forbidden Desert. One or two markers per tile is manageable. Three or more is a crisis. The Archaeologist should focus on the sandiest areas because they clear two markers per action. Everyone else should chip in when passing through. A good rule of thumb: never end your turn leaving a tile above two sand markers if you are adjacent to it.

walkthrough.title: Water Conservation
walkthrough.text: Water is finite and Sun Beats Down cards drain everyone simultaneously. Position players near tunnel tiles whenever possible because tunnels provide shelter. The Water Carrier is your most important player. Keep them near the team center so they can share water quickly. Do not waste actions having the Water Carrier excavate random tiles. Their job is water logistics.

walkthrough.title: Use Your Role Powers
walkthrough.text: Each role has a powerful ability that should be used every turn if possible. The Explorer's diagonal movement lets them reach tiles others cannot. The Navigator's ability to move other players saves the team actions. The Archaeologist's double sand removal is essential for keeping paths clear. The Meteorologist can reduce storm card draws, slowing the storm. Do not play generically. Lean into your role's strength.

walkthrough.title: Tunnel Network
walkthrough.text: Excavated tunnel tiles are incredibly valuable. They provide shelter from Sun Beats Down and allow free travel between any two tunnel entrances. Once you have two or more tunnels excavated, your team can move across the board efficiently. Prioritize excavating tunnel tiles early because they pay dividends for the rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: Storm Card Awareness
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to which storm cards have been drawn. Track how many Storm Picks Up and Sun Beats Down cards have appeared. If most Sun Beats Down cards are already drawn, you can be more aggressive about positioning outside tunnels. If the storm meter is climbing quickly, accelerate your part collection because time is running out.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Coordination
walkthrough.text: Once all four parts are collected, you need every player on the launch pad. This is where games are won or lost. Start positioning players toward the launch pad as soon as the third part is collected. The Navigator can move teammates efficiently. Save a Jet Pack card for emergency transport. Do not wait until all parts are collected to start converging. Plan ahead.

Splitting too far from Water Carrier's reach
Hoarding equipment instead of using it proactively
Chasing parts before both clue tiles are revealed

walkthrough.title: Optimal Exploration Pattern
walkthrough.text: Advanced teams explore in a systematic pattern rather than randomly. Start by clearing the tiles adjacent to the crash site, then spiral outward. This minimizes backtracking and ensures you find clue tiles efficiently. The Explorer and Archaeologist push outward while the Water Carrier and Navigator support from behind. Cover the board methodically rather than chasing individual tiles.

walkthrough.title: Storm Eye Tracking
walkthrough.text: The storm eye moves when wind cards are drawn. Advanced players track the storm eye's position and avoid excavating tiles near it because those tiles will receive the most sand. Instead, explore tiles on the far side of the board from the storm eye. Sand tends to accumulate in predictable patterns based on the eye's movement.

walkthrough.title: Water Carrier Optimization
walkthrough.text: The Water Carrier's ability to revisit excavated well tiles is incredibly powerful. Advanced teams position the Water Carrier near a well tile and have them shuttle water back and forth. The Water Carrier spends one action filling up at the well and one action sharing water with a teammate. This creates a sustainable water pipeline that keeps the team alive longer than any other strategy.

walkthrough.title: Meteorologist Storm Management
walkthrough.text: The Meteorologist can spend actions to draw fewer storm cards at the end of their turn or peek at upcoming storm cards. Advanced teams have the Meteorologist spend one or two actions per turn reducing storm card draws, especially when the storm meter is at four or five. This effectively slows the game's clock, giving the team more time to find parts. The trade-off is fewer excavation actions, but the time saved is usually worth it.

walkthrough.title: Navigator as Force Multiplier
walkthrough.text: The Navigator can move another player up to three spaces as a single action. This is equivalent to giving another player three extra actions worth of movement. Advanced teams use the Navigator to teleport the Archaeologist to sand-heavy areas and the Water Carrier to thirsty teammates. The Navigator often spends most of their actions moving others rather than excavating, acting as a team coordinator.

walkthrough.title: Equipment Timing
walkthrough.text: While general advice says to use equipment early, advanced teams time equipment for maximum impact. The Dune Blaster is best when a critical path has three or more sand markers. The Jet Pack is saved for getting the last player to the launch pad. The Time Throttle is used when one player needs six actions in a turn to complete a critical task. The key is proactive timing, not reactive panic.

walkthrough.title: Sand Triage
walkthrough.text: When sand is accumulating faster than you can clear it, you need to triage. Focus sand clearing only on tiles that are on the direct path to clue tiles, machine parts, or the launch pad. Let non-essential tiles bury themselves. Clearing sand on a random corner tile while a critical path is blocked is a wasted action. Ruthless prioritization wins Elite and Legendary difficulty.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Sprint Planning
walkthrough.text: The final phase of the game is a sprint to the launch pad. Advanced teams plan this sprint several turns in advance. Count the number of actions needed to get every player to the launch pad. Factor in the Navigator's movement ability and any remaining Jet Packs. Coordinate so that the last part is picked up by a player already near the launch pad. The team that plans the sprint earliest wins the most games.

summary.title: Optimal Exploration Pattern
bullets: Spiral outward from crash site rather than random exploration
Minimizes backtracking, ensures efficient clue finding
Explorer/Archaeologist push out; Water Carrier/Navigator support
Methodical coverage > chasing individual tiles

summary.title: Storm Eye Tracking
bullets: Track storm eye position as wind cards shift tiles
Avoid excavating near the storm eye (highest sand accumulation)
Explore far side of board from storm eye
Sand patterns are predictable based on eye movement

Creates sustainable water pipeline for the team
Most effective water strategy at higher difficulties

Fewer excavations, but time saved is usually worth it
Critical when storm meter is at 4-5

Navigator often moves others more than themselves

entries.definition: Adventurer role. Can move and remove sand diagonally in addition to orthogonally. Starting water: 4. Adds flexibility in movement and sand clearing.
Adventurer role. Can spend actions to draw fewer storm cards at end of turn or peek at upcoming storm cards. Starting water: 4. Slows the storm's progression.
Adventurer role. Can move another player up to 3 spaces as a single action. Starting water: 4. Acts as a team coordinator and force multiplier.
Adventurer role. Starting water: 5. Can take water from excavated well tiles and give water to adjacent players as an action. The team's primary water source.
Adventurer role. Can move onto and through tiles with 2 or more sand markers (blocked tiles) and carry one other player along. Starting water: 3.
Adventurer role. Removes 2 sand markers per action instead of the normal 1. Starting water: 3. Essential for keeping paths clear.
The empty center space in the 5x5 grid. Tiles slide around this space when wind cards are drawn. The storm eye effectively moves as tiles shift.
Stackable sand piece placed on tiles. Tiles with 1+ sand cannot be excavated. Tiles with 2+ sand are blocked (impassable except by Climber). If sand supply runs out, the team loses.
Tracks storm intensity from 2 to the skull (loss). Determines how many storm cards are drawn per turn. Advances when Storm Picks Up cards are drawn. Cannot be reversed.
When excavated, shows the row or column location for one machine part. Each part has 2 clue tiles. When both are revealed, the part appears at the intersection.
When excavated, draw equipment cards equal to the number shown (1 or 2). Equipment cards provide powerful abilities.
When excavated, players on or adjacent to the tile gain 2 water (up to max). Water Carrier can revisit excavated water tiles for additional water.
When excavated, provides shelter from Sun Beats Down cards and allows travel between any two tunnel entrances as a single move action. Extremely valuable.
When excavated, looks like a water tile but provides no water. A trap that wastes an excavation.
The tile where all players must gather with all 4 machine parts to win the game. Protecting and reaching the launch pad is the ultimate goal.
Storm card showing 1-3 directional arrows. Shifts tiles in the indicated direction. After shifting, 1 sand marker is placed on each tile that moved.
Storm card. All players not on a tunnel tile or protected by Solar Shield lose 1 water. No tile shifting occurs. Most dangerous card type.
Storm card. Advances storm meter 1 level, increasing future storm card draws. No tile shifting. Permanently increases difficulty.
Equipment card. Remove all sand markers from one tile. Free to use (no action cost). Best for clearing heavily buried critical tiles.
Equipment card. Move yourself or another player to any unblocked tile on the board. Free to use. Best saved for emergency rescue or endgame launch pad convergence.
Equipment card. Secretly look at any face-down tile without excavating it. Free to use. Helps plan exploration efficiently.
Equipment card. Protects you from the next Sun Beats Down card. Free to use. Play proactively when you expect Sun Beats Down soon.
Equipment card. Gain 2 water for any player. Free to use. Emergency hydration for a dehydrating teammate.
Equipment card. Take 2 extra actions immediately on your current turn. Free to use. Best for critical turns requiring 6 total actions.
Four parts that must be collected to win: Propeller, Engine, Navigation Deck, and Crystal. Located by excavating both clue tiles for each part.
